title: euphrasia nemorosa subsp. coerulea
intro: Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. coerulea, also known as blue marsh gentian or blue mountain gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal various health issues. The health benefits of Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. coerulea include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to support eye health, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. coerulea can be made into tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
